prompt:
Pharmaceutical vial product photography, vertical 4:5 portrait composition. The reference image shows the locked vial geometry of a 5ml round BPC-157 vial, navy blue cap (hex #101850), studio lighting, label material, and bottom accent bar — reproduce all of these identically. Cap color must be EXACTLY hex #101850. Vial size: 5ml, exact proportions matching the reference.

The label has the following content laid out top to bottom: (1) Upper roughly 62 percent of the label is a blank pristine white logo zone (slightly more empty space than the reference) — absolutely no graphics, text, lines, or marks of any kind. The text block below should sit a TOUCH LOWER on the label than the reference — push the entire text block down by a small amount so there's slightly more empty white space above 'BPC-157' and slightly less white space below 'LOT: NH-1024'. The product name 'BPC-157' should still sit clearly above the label's vertical midpoint, just dropped a few pixels lower than the reference.

LOCKED TYPEFACE FOR ALL TEXT: Helvetica Neue. (2) Product name 'BPC-157' rendered in HELVETICA NEUE BOLD (~700) in dark charcoal grey, perfectly horizontally centered, natural letter proportions. (3) Directly below the product name with tight spacing, the dose-and-volume line 'xx mg · 5 ml' rendered in HELVETICA NEUE BOLD (same bold weight) at roughly 60 percent of the product-name size, charcoal grey, centered, with a single middle-dot separator. The 'xx' must be rendered LITERALLY as two lowercase letter x characters — intentional placeholder, not a number. (4) Below the dose with a small visual gap, two stacked compliance lines in HELVETICA NEUE LIGHT (~300, NOT bold), all-caps, medium grey, ~22 percent of the product-name size, tight line spacing. Line 1: 'FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY'. Line 2: 'NOT FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION'. (5) Below the second compliance line with a small gap, the lot line 'LOT: NH-1024' in HELVETICA NEUE LIGHT, same small size, medium grey, single line, perfectly horizontally centered. (6) At the very bottom edge of the label, the slim solid navy accent bar at exactly hex #101850 must run UNINTERRUPTED EDGE-TO-EDGE across the full width of the label. Match the reference's hairline (if any) above the bar exactly. Absolutely NO additional hairlines anywhere. Sharp focus, high resolution, clinical minimalist aesthetic. CRITICAL: render the dose line literally as 'xx mg · 5 ml' with two lowercase x's; do NOT replace 'xx' with a number.

Pharmaceutical vial product photography, vertical 4:5 portrait composition. TWO REFERENCE IMAGES are provided. The FIRST reference is the LABEL / TYPOGRAPHY / CAP ANCHOR — a 3ml clear-glass BPC-157 baseline showing the locked label layout, typography hierarchy, navy cap (hex #101850), bottom accent bar, lighting, and shadow style. Reproduce the label content, typeface, alignment, sizing, hairline, and bar EXACTLY from this first reference. The SECOND reference is the VIAL GLASS ANCHOR — an empty amber/dark-brown 3ml glass vial. Reproduce the vial's amber tinted glass color, geometry, and proportions EXACTLY from this second reference. The vial must be the same compact 3ml size and silhouette as the second reference, NOT taller or chunkier. Cap color must remain EXACTLY hex #101850 navy. Vial size: 3ml dark amber glass.

LABEL MATERIAL — CRITICAL: the label face must be rendered as pristine opaque matte white paper / vinyl, completely uniform across its entire surface. The label must be FULLY OPAQUE — absolutely NO translucency, NO transparency, NO see-through to the amber glass behind it. There must be NO dark spots, NO low-opacity shadowy patches, NO amber tint bleeding through, NO blotches, NO ghosting, NO discoloration anywhere on the label face. The white must read clean, even, and pristine across the whole label surface. Only natural soft photographic shadow falloff at the very edges where the label curves around the vial cylinder is acceptable; the central label face must be uniformly clean white.

The label has the following content laid out top to bottom (exactly matching the first reference): (1) Upper roughly 60 percent of the label is a blank pristine white logo zone with absolutely no graphics, text, lines, or marks of any kind. (2) Product name 'BPC-157' rendered in HELVETICA NEUE BOLD (~700) in dark charcoal grey, perfectly horizontally centered, natural letter proportions. (3) Directly below with tight spacing, the dose-and-volume line 'xx mg · 3 ml' rendered in HELVETICA NEUE BOLD (same bold weight) at roughly 60 percent of the product-name size, charcoal grey, centered, with a single middle-dot separator. 'xx' must be two lowercase letter x characters — intentional placeholder, not a number. (4) Below the dose with a small visual gap, two stacked compliance lines in HELVETICA NEUE LIGHT (~300, NOT bold), all-caps, medium grey. Line 1: 'FOR RESEARCH USE ONLY'. Line 2: 'NOT FOR HUMAN CONSUMPTION'. (5) Below the second compliance line, the lot line 'LOT: NH-1024' in HELVETICA NEUE LIGHT, same small size, medium grey, single line, perfectly centered. (6) BAR: at the very bottom edge of the label, the slim solid navy accent bar at exactly hex #101850 must run UNINTERRUPTED EDGE-TO-EDGE across the full width of the label, from the absolute leftmost to the absolute rightmost edge — NO gap, NO truncation, NO fade, NO white space at either end. Match the first reference's hairline above the bar exactly. Absolutely NO additional hairlines. Sharp focus, high resolution, clinical minimalist aesthetic. CRITICAL: render every text string exactly as written including 'xx mg · 3 ml' literally with two lowercase x's. CRITICAL: label face must be uniformly opaque clean white with NO dark patches or tinting from the amber glass behind it.
